description: Sector overlay for accounting firms, audit practices, assurance providers, financial reporting professionals, and related professional-services documentation in the UK.

metadata.scope: UK sector overlay controls for Accounting. Intended to be used with uk_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official UK legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.
metadata.territorial_scope_note: UK law can differ across England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland. This starter pack uses UK-wide sources where available and flags devolved or nation-specific application for human review.

primary_sources.name: FRS 102 The Financial Reporting Standard applicable in the UK and Republic of Ireland
Money Laundering Regulations 2017 / AML supervisory materials
used_for: AML obligations for relevant professional services

rules.title: Companies Act accounts and statutory-reporting evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for annual accounts, directors' report, strategic report, audit exemption, or Companies Act reporting evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: UK GAAP / FRS reporting framework evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for UK GAAP, FRS 102, FRS 101, FRS 105, accounting policy, or disclosure evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: ISA (UK) or audit methodology evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for audit plan, audit evidence, ISAs (UK), audit methodology, quality management, or engagement review evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Auditor independence and ethical-standard evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for independence, ethical standard, conflict of interest, non-audit services, fee dependence, or objectivity evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: AML/client due diligence evidence is present where accountancy services are in scope
rules.summary: Checks for AML risk assessment, CDD, beneficial ownership, sanctions screening, or suspicious activity procedures.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
